steps.description: Aqueous 15 nm citrate-capped Au nanoparticles were used as seeds due to their colloidal stability, compatibility with the SnCl4 precursor, and the labile nature of the citrate capping agent.

Seeded chemical reduction of Sn4+ onto Au nanoparticle seeds. The incorporation of Sn was controlled by modifying the added Sn amount. Synthesis at 60°C was found to give the most blue-shifted LSPR and the greatest relative amount of Au5Sn phase.

synthesis.notes: The approach yields uniform, spherical, and monodisperse (<15% coefficient of variation) nanoparticles for all compositions investigated. Elemental mapping shows that Sn is homogeneously distributed. XRD reveals the formation of Au5Sn and AuSn intermetallic phases with increasing Sn content, coexisting with a substitutional AuxSn1-x alloy phase. Temperature-dependent experiments showed that 60°C gives the most blue-shifted LSPR; lower temperatures (40°C and room temperature) resulted in broadened LSPRs with minimal blue-shift and greater AuSn fractions; at 80°C, minimal shifting was observed.

steps.description: Aqueous 15 nm citrate-capped Au nanoparticles were used as seeds due to their colloidal stability, compatibility with the SnCl4 precursor, and the labile nature of the citrate capping agent.

Seeded chemical reduction of Sn4+ was performed. Synthesis at 60°C was found to give the most blue-shifted LSPR and the greatest relative amount of Au5Sn phase. The incorporation of Sn was controlled by modifying the added Sn amount.

synthesis.notes: Au-Sn nanoparticles were synthesized using a seeded chemical reduction of Sn4+. The Au5Sn intermetallic phase (trigonal) was observed at up to 21% Sn content by XRD. Rietveld analysis suggests that increasing Sn content leads to a monotonic increase in Au5Sn formation for 8, 21, and 25% Sn incorporation. The most blue-shifted LSPRs occur when Au/AuxSn1-x and intermetallic (Au5Sn + AuSn) content are present. Synthesis at 60°C gave the greatest relative amount of Au5Sn phase. ICP-MS and EDS were used to quantify Sn incorporation.

synthesis.notes: The synthesis is based on a seeded chemical reduction approach adapted from the Au-Sn synthesis, with modified incubation and alloying temperatures and 10% molar equivalent PTM addition. ICP-MS determined Bi content to be ~6%. The resulting nanoparticles are spherical and homogeneous in size (<10% coefficient of variation). STEM-EDS maps show relatively uniform composition. XRD shows a Au-like fcc pattern suggestive of substitutional alloying with no intermetallic phases observed. The LSPR blue-shifts to 504 nm compared to 520 nm for Au seeds.

Seeded chemical reduction of Sn4+ precursor (SnCl4) onto the Au seeds.

steps.description: Use aqueous 15 nm citrate-capped Au nanoparticles as seeds.
Add SnCl4 precursor. The incorporation of Sn is controlled by modifying the added Sn amount.
Perform incubation and alloying steps. Temperature is varied to manipulate diffusion of Sn into Au seed.
Seeded chemical reduction of Sn4+ to form Au-Sn alloy nanoparticles.

synthesis.notes: Synthesis at 60°C yields the most blue-shifted LSPR and greatest relative amount of Au5Sn phase. Lower temperatures (40°C, RT) result in broadened LSPRs and greater fractions of AuSn. At 80°C, minimal shifting is observed. Sn content is quantified by ICP-MS and EDS (8%, 21%, 25%, 41% Sn compositions achieved).

steps.description: Prepare 15 nm citrate-capped Au nanoparticles as seeds in aqueous solution
Add SnCl4 precursor to the Au seed solution with controlled Sn amount to achieve desired composition
Heat the reaction mixture at 60°C for optimal Au5Sn phase formation (up to 21% Sn content)
Allow Sn diffusion into Au seeds to form Au5Sn intermetallic phase through seeded chemical reduction

synthesis.notes: Au5Sn intermetallic phase forms at Sn content up to 21%. Synthesis at 60°C gives the greatest relative amount of Au5Sn phase. Lower temperatures (40°C and room temperature) result in greater fractions of AuSn instead. The Au5Sn phase is trigonal structure. XRD confirms Au5Sn formation alongside retained Au fcc pattern, suggesting coexistence with substitutional AuxSn1-x alloy phase.

steps.description: Prepare aqueous 15 nm citrate-capped Au nanoparticles as seeds for colloidal stability and compatibility with SnCl4 precursor
Add SnCl4 precursor to the Au seed solution with controlled Sn amount to modify incorporation percentage

Incubate and alloy at controlled temperature. 60°C gives most blue-shifted LSPR and greatest Au5Sn phase. Lower temperatures (40°C and room temperature) lead to greater fractions of AuSn phase. At 25% and 41% Sn content, hexagonal AuSn phase is observed.
Sn diffusion occurs throughout the nanoparticle dimensions, resulting in homogeneous distribution. For AuSn phase formation, higher Sn content (25-41%) is required.

synthesis.notes: The synthesis produces uniform, spherical, and monodisperse nanoparticles (<15% coefficient of variation). Sn content can be controlled from 8% to 41%. At 25% and 41% Sn incorporation, the hexagonal AuSn intermetallic phase is observed by XRD. The Au fcc pattern is retained in all cases with Vegard's-like shifting. Lower temperatures (40°C and room temperature) lead to formation of greater fractions of AuSn phase compared to 60°C which favors Au5Sn. The optical response is stable for at least two weeks.

steps.description: Seeded chemical reduction of Sn4+ onto Au nanoparticles.
Temperature-dependent experiments conducted at 60°C for optimal Sn diffusion and Au5Sn phase formation.

synthesis.notes: The incorporation of Sn was controlled by modifying the added Sn amount. Nanoparticles were uniform, spherical, and monodisperse (<15% coefficient of variation). At low Sn content, substitutional alloying occurs; at higher Sn content, intermetallic phases (Au5Sn, AuSn) form.
